title: hibbertia orientalis
intro: Hibbertia orientalis, also known as the Geebung, is a small shrub native to eastern Australia, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and leathery leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the severity of colds and flu. Consuming the plant's leaves or using its extracts in herbal preparations may provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Hibbertia orientalis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used in cooking or as a garnish in salads.
